name: Chocolate Lover's Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 packages (8 oz each) cream cheese, softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine graham cracker crumbs and 1/3 cup sugar. Stir in melted margarine or butter. Press into the bottom and halfway up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
2. Blend cream cheese, cocoa, and 3/4 cup sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs and vanilla until smooth. Add chocolate chips. Pour batter into springform pan.
3. Bake at 375°F for 20 minutes.
4. Let cheesecake cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes.
5. Preheat oven to 425°F.
6. Blend sour cream, 2 tbsp sugar, and vanilla until smooth. Spread topping over chocolate portion of cake.
7. Bake at 425°F for an additional 10 minutes.
8. Cool on wire rack. Loosen cake from sides of pan with a knife or spatula. When completely cool, remove sides of pan. Refrigerate before serving. Store leftovers in the refrigerator.
I almost burned out the motor on my cheap hand-held mixer trying to blend the cream cheese mixture. It was better for me to use a wooden spoon when combining the dry ingredients and then use the mixer with the eggs.
This is based on memory, but we made this recipe 2 weekends in a row, so I am confident that the measurements are correct. The oven temperatures were also confirmed as correct.
While not requested, cherries would be a nice addition to this cheesecake.
